name: Border Shift Log
pages:
Border post 14, night shift.

Lights failed on the eastern fence at 02:10. Patrol heard movement past the old logging road but found only torn survey tape and fresh tracks in the ditch. Snowfall covered the prints before dawn.

Command says the line is closed until further notice. The men keep asking who is on the other side now. I told them to count batteries, not rumors.

Signed: Sgt. A. Koivu

name: Evacuation Notice
pages:
Municipal notice, copied by hand.

All residents east of the clinic road must report to the school gymnasium with identification, medicine, warm clothing, and food for two days. Pets are permitted only if contained.
Do not return for property after registration. The buses will not wait for missing persons. If your household is already empty, mark the front door with chalk so rescue teams do not force entry.

name: Clinic Triage Card
pages:
Name unreadable. Male, approx. 40.

Exposure, lacerations on both hands, possible concussion. Patient repeats that the checkpoint siren sounded after the bridge was already gone.

No transport available. Gave iodine, blanket, and half dose of pain medicine. Told him to stay awake until morning.

Morning note: bed empty, blanket folded.

name: Forest Cache Note
pages:
If you are reading this, you found the wrong stump.

The real cache is not under the mossy one by the creek. That is where I put empty tins for thieves with more hunger than patience.
Look uphill for three birches grown from one root. Dig on the side facing the old tower. Take only what you need and put the stones back exactly as they were.

name: Rail Manifest Fragment
pages:
Freight yard transfer sheet, torn at bottom.

Wagon 03: tools, dry batteries, water filters.
Wagon 04: medical cartons, winter blankets.
Wagon 05: sealed crates, no civilian inventory.
The dispatcher stamped the convoy as delayed, but we heard it leave before sunrise with no lights. Someone scraped the destination code off every crate in wagon 05.

name: Letter to Leena
pages:
Leena,

I left the compass in the blue cup, not because I think you need it, but because you always said the cabin turned people around. North is still north, even here.
Do not wait for me after Thursday. If the boats are running, go with your sister. I will follow when the road opens, and if it does not, remember me before the sirens.

name: Patrol Warning
pages:
Tape this inside every patrol locker.

Do not pursue signal flares beyond the quarry road. Do not answer single knocks on checkpoint doors after curfew. Do not split pairs to investigate crying.
If you find white cloth tied to a gate, mark the location and withdraw. The last team treated it as a surrender marker. We recovered one rifle and no bodies.

name: Schoolroom List
pages:
Classroom supplies for shelter use:

Chalk, tape, scissors, old maps, sports mats, blankets from storage, batteries from science room, radio from headmaster office.

Names checked in: 47.
Names reunited: 31.
Names moved north: 12.
Names still listening for parents at the windows: 4.

name: Radio Transcript
pages:
Recorded on emergency channel 6.

03:42: Unknown station requests road status.
03:44: Border command orders silence.
03:47: Unknown station repeats the names of three missing patrolmen.

03:51: Carrier wave, then music from an old children's broadcast.
03:53: Transmission ends with two words in Finnish: do not open.

name: Weathered Recipe
pages:
Pea soup for when everyone arrives late.

Soak peas before sunrise. Add onion if there is onion. Add pork if somebody is proud. Add water when the pot starts sounding angry.
Serves six on a good day and twelve during bad news. Keep stirring after the first siren. Food burns quickly when people stop watching it.

name: Smuggler's Ledger
pages:
Run 14. Border at km 7, Tuesday, moonless.

Ryzhiy carries the soft load. Mäkki drives. The third one — no. DO NOT bring him this time. He talks to his wife.

Owed: 400 € to the cousin in Kotka, 60,000 ₽ to nobody you ask about, two cartons to the man at the gate who isn't from here.

Don't write the name of the truck.

If the lights on the eastern fence are out, that means they want us through. If the lights are on, that means they want to see us. Both mean the same thing now.

Burn this if you find it before me.

name: Letter from Tampere
pages:
My boy,

The candle by the window is the same candle. I light it at six and again before bed, like always, so you know which window is ours if you come back at night.

The Virtanen boy came home last Thursday. He is thinner. He sat with his mother for an hour and did not speak. The other one did not come.

Do not write back if it costs you to send a letter. I would rather you keep the stamp.

Eat what you can. Sleep when there is sleep. The pear tree by the shed has the best year I can remember and there will be enough for both of us if you hurry.

— Mama

name: Hamina Dispatch
pages:
Port of Hamina — Pier 4 Control, Shift Log
Juhannusaatto. Watch: K. Lehto.

20:00 — Relief on duty. Bay quiet, no traffic since 18:40.
21:15 — Foreman walks the line. Two pallets resecured at berth.
22:10 — Reefer on container 7 trips again. Reset, holding.
22:34 — Wind SSW 6 m/s. Solvent smell off the water — bilge from the tug at 3?

22:38 — Wind W 9 m/s. Wrong. Recheck mast.
22:40 — Mast reads SSW. Sleeve on the pole reads W. Both fresh. Called the office, no answer.
22:41 — Klaxon at Pier 4 just went —

name: Guard's Notebook
pages:
K-9. Two more shots from K-12 last night. The new ones don't sleep.

K-12 says it was a deer. Twice. With a rifle they're not supposed to have past dark.

Petrov found a casing at K-11. .308. Pocketed it before I saw.

K-7 fence cut clean. Wire pliers, not chewed. Reported up. No reply.

Old hands stop talking when the new ones walk in. New ones stop talking when we do.

Asked for a transfer in March. Asked again in May. Stamped received. Nothing else.

name: Crayon Map
pages:
MY HOUSE

door
my BED. me and TUOMAS
MAMA WORK desk
LIVING. tv broke
the BLACK ROOM
BAFROOM. window

stairs go DOWN

dont go in the kitchen
mama said

mama is sleeping in the LIVING
she is tired

at night it sounds like the tap
but the tap is off
i checked

name: Posted Orders
pages:
ORDER NO. 7
KYMENLAAKSO REGIONAL ADMINISTRATION
[stamp: legible]

To all civilian residents of Area 05:

Remain in your residence. Close all
windows. Await transport at your
[obscured]
within 48 hours.

Do not [obscured] west.
Do not gather at [obscured].

Issued: 22.06
Signed: District Coordinator

ORDER NO. 11
[stamp: unfamiliar — bilingual seal]

To all civilians remaining in Area 05:

Evacuate west on foot before sunset.
Do not wait for transport.
Carry no more than you can run with.

Routes: Highway E18 west.
Do not use rail.

Issued: 29.06
By authority of the [obscured] command.

name: Triage Tag
pages:
SCHOOL MEDICAL STN. — CHART FRAG.
Date: [tear]/07
Patient: UNKNOWN MALE
Age: ~30
Adm: 02:14

GSW R-shoulder, thru-and-thru.
Frag wound L-thigh, lodged.
Pulse 124. BP 90/60.
Conscious, non-verbal.

Tx: pressure dressing R-sh.
Tourniquet L-thigh, 02:18.
Morphine 10mg IM. (Last vial.)

Att.: V. Mäkinen

03:40 — pt. stable, sleeping.
04:55 — checked. fine.

[different hand:]

Patient disappeared before morning rounds.
No bed. No tag. No blood trail.
Door to staff exit was bolted from inside.

— K.
